channel.title: Pro Running News
channel.description: Pro Running News is a new podcast series covering the current events of the professional running scene hosted by Matt Fox (Sweat Elite Founder) and David Lipman (Exercise Physiologist and Medical Doctor)

item.title: E56 - Whereabouts Failures and The US Marathon Trials
item.description: Dave and Matt discuss some Athletics Integrity Unit news this week with one of the world's best 1500m and 5000m runners handed a suspension. Dave and Matt also discuss the US Olympic Marathon trials.
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary: Dave and Matt discuss some Athletics Integrity Unit news this week with one of the world's best 1500m and 5000m runners handed a suspension. Dave and Matt also discuss the US Olympic Marathon trials.

item.title: E55 - US Olympic Marathon Trials Preview
item.description: One of the most competitive Marathons in the United States of America takes place this weekend - the USA Olympic Marathon Trials. Both the womens and the mens fields are strong- Dave and Matt discuss the favourites, the dark horses and share some predictions.
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary: One of the most competitive Marathons in the United States of America takes place this weekend - the USA Olympic Marathon Trials. Both the womens and the mens fields are strong- Dave and Matt discuss the favourites, the dark horses and share some predictions.

item.title: E54 - Choosing Your Next Marathon
item.description: A listener asks about racing the marathon and how to select your next one. Dave and Matt discuss.
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary: A listener asks about racing the marathon and how to select your next one. Dave and Matt discuss.

item.title: E53 - Hendrik Pfeiffer's Remarkable Road To A 2:07 Marathon
item.description: Hendrik Pfeiffer ran 2:07:14 at the Houston Marathon last weekend to run an Olympic Qualifying time and become the 4th fastest German of all time. Hendrik raced 4 marathons between April 2023 and January 2014- running 2:12 or faster in all 4. He also raced 5 Marathons in 2022. Dave and Matt discuss.
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary: Hendrik Pfeiffer ran 2:07:14 at the Houston Marathon last weekend to run an Olympic Qualifying time and become the 4th fastest German of all time. Hendrik raced 4 marathons between April 2023 and January 2014- running 2:12 or faster in all 4. He also raced 5 Marathons in 2022. Dave and Matt discuss.

item.title: E52 - How Important is VO2Max
item.description:
What is VO2max? Is Garmin VO2Max accurate? How much is genetic vs trainable? What contributes to running performance? Dave and Matt discuss vo2 Max and it's importance in running.


Show Notes:

Article on Genes and Trainability of VO2max - 

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5688475/ 

Article on Breaking 2 Project Physiology - 

https://journals.physiology.org/doi/full/10.1152/japplphysiol.00647.2020?rfr_dat=cr_pub++0pubmed&url_ver=Z39.88-2003&rfr_id=ori%3Arid%3Acrossref.org 

Article on What it Would take to Break a 2hr marathon - 

https://journals.physiology.org/doi/full/10.1152/japplphysiol.00563.2010?rfr_dat=cr_pub++0pubmed&url_ver=Z39.88-2003&rfr_id=ori%3Arid%3Acrossref.org 

Article on Marathon Physiology - 

https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fcvm.2022.856875/full#B1

item.title: E51 - Social Media & Runners - What is Influence?
item.description:
In this episode Matt and Dave discuss social media and runners, prompted by an article in Trailrunner Magazine about influencers vs pros and some recent discussion of the topic.


Show Notes

Article Mentioned on Influencers vs Pros 

https://www.trailrunnermag.com/people/culture-people/where-is-the-line-between-athlete-and-influencer/

item.title: E50 - Youth or Experience in Running, which dominates?
item.description: In this episode of Pro Running News Matt and Dave discuss age and running courtesy of a listener question. Does the recovery ability of youth trump the durability and experience of age?
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary: In this episode of Pro Running News Matt and Dave discuss age and running courtesy of a listener question. Does the recovery ability of youth trump the durability and experience of age?

item.title: E49 - Ekiden - The Most Watched Running Event Annually
item.description:
In Episode 49 Dave and Matt discuss the build up to Ekiden - the world's most watched running event that happens every year between January 1 and 3.


Interesting links: https://www.jrpass.com/blog/the-guide-to-the-incredible-sport-of-ekiden
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary:

item.title: E48 - Valencia Marathon 2023 Recap
item.description:
Another year on and Valencia is still arguably the fastest marathon in the world.

Covering all the regular things you'd expect:

Race coverage, results, records, tactics, who would be happy, who'd be unhappy and everything else.
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary:
Another year on and Valencia is still arguably the fastest marathon in the world.

Covering all the regular things you'd expect:

Race coverage, results, records, tactics, who would be happy, who'd be unhappy and everything else.

item.title: E47 - How Often Should You Have Rest Days & Deload Weeks?
item.description:
A listener of the show asks about burn out and taking recovery days and weeks. Dave and Matt discuss the question and answers in detail.


We recently reached over 100 reviews on Spotify with a 4.9 rating. Thank you to all who have rated the podcast positively!


Show Notes:

Articles on HRV Guided Training - 

https://www.researchgate.net/publication/5991670_Endurance_training_guided_individually_by_daily_heart_rate_variability_measurements

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29809080/

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/abs/pii/S0031938421003413

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8507742/

item.title: E46 - The USA Olympic Marathon Trials
item.description: There's been some recent discussions around the USA Olympic Marathon Trials start time changing with a majority of athletes proposing to USATF to change the start time from mid day to earlier in the day. Dave and Matt discuss the USA Olympic Marathon trials in details.
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
item.itunes:summary: There's been some recent discussions around the USA Olympic Marathon Trials start time changing with a majority of athletes proposing to USATF to change the start time from mid day to earlier in the day. Dave and Matt discuss the USA Olympic Marathon trials in details.

item.title: E45 - Bodyweight and RED-S
item.description:
A listener of the show asks about balancing weight loss with training and the possible effects of RED-S and how to avoid it. Dave and Matt discuss.


Show Notes - 

Article on LEA and use of Continuous Glucose Monitors - 

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40279-023-01910-4 

Article on Prevalence of LEA - 

https://www.abstractsonline.com/pp8/?utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email#!/10799/presentation/3704 

RED-S BMJ Article - 

https://bjsm.bmj.com/content/57/17/1073 

Article on Body Composition Changes Across a Season in Elite Athlete Case Study - 

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29140157/ 

Article on Evolutionary Aspects of Physical Performance in Energy Deficit - 

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S109564332300106X 

Article on Injury Free Time and Performance - 

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/26839047/ 

Project RED-S IG - 

https://instagram.com/project_reds_?igshid=NGVhN2U2NjQ0Yg==

item.title: Ep 44 - New York Marathon - A Tale of 2 Races
item.description:
Dave and Matt discuss the 2023 New York Marathon in detail - reflecting on both the women's and men's elite fields, results, commentary, DNF's and more.


We'd love it if you could rate the podcast for us on your podcast player. Thank you!

item.title: Ep 43 - Can you use Protein Instead of Carbs?
item.description:
Anne Haug - 2nd place finisher at the World Ironman Championships last month shared that she uses Protein in the form of Amino Acids to fuel during races rather than Carbohydrates which is what this episode is all about - Dave and Matt discuss.


Show Notes:

Article on Anne Haug’s Kona Fueling - 

https://www.tri247.com/triathlon-news/elite/anne-haug-ironman-world-championship-kona-2023-reaction-analysis-nutrition

Link to Dan Plews’ Post About his Marathon Nutrition - 

https://www.instagram.com/p/Cy2UyTeuwbb/ 

Article on Amino Acid Metabolism - 

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8015690/ 

Articles on Amino Acids Metabolism During Exercise - 

https://journals.lww.com/acsm-msse/abstract/1987/10001/amino_acid_and_protein_metabolism_during_exercise.9.aspx 

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/10940342/#:~:text=Sustained%20dynamic%20exercise%20stimulates%20amino,or%20both)%3B%20some%20of 

https://link.springer.com/article/10.2165/00007256-199009010-00003 

https://www.nature.com/articles/s42255-020-0251-4 

Wikipedia Page on Gluconeogenesis - 

https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gluconeogenesis#/media/File:Amino_acid_catabolism_revised.png 

Article on the Role of Fat Oxidation - 

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5117036/ 

Article on Metabolic Changes with a Low Carb Diet - 

https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fphys.2023.1150265/full 

Rothschild Article on Pre-exercise Intake -

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33198277/

item.title: Ep 42 - Are your Competitors Doping?
item.description:
Dave and Matt discuss Kenyan 2:02 Marathoner Titus Ekiru being banned for 10 years as well as a disturbing study about doping in recreational sports.

[Apologies about the poor audio on Matt's side on this episode]


Show Notes

Marathoner Banned for Doping - 

https://www.reuters.com/sports/athletics/kenyan-marathon-runner-ekiru-receives-10-year-doping-ban-2023-10-16/

Article on Doping Prevalence -

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40279-017-0765-4 

Article on Triathlon doping Prevalence - 

https://journals.plos.org/plosone/article/file?id=10.1371%2Fjournal.pone.0078702&type=printable 

WADA Survey - 

https://www.wada-ama.org/sites/default/files/2022-03/Descriptive%20Report%20-%20Athlete%20Vulnerabilties%20-%2023-03-2022.pdf

Article on Attitudes Towards Doping in Elite Adolescents - 

https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S1440244021001456

item.title: E41 - Is the Half Marathon Disrespected?
item.description:
Dave and Matt discuss the Half Marathon, the relatively new series "SuperHalf's" that has recently had the Berlin Half Marathon join and the reasons why many elite Marathoner's do not race the Half Marathon.


Show Notes:

Super halves Website-

https://www.superhalfs.com/en/

item.title: E40 - Chicago Marathon - Echos of London & Pacing Lessons
item.description:
The 2023 Chicago Marathon featured a men's marathon world record, the 2nd fastest female marathon of all time and plenty of surprises. Dave and Matt break down the historic event that was the 2023 Chicago Marathon.

Show Notes:

Mens Results - 

https://results.chicagomarathon.com/2023/?pid=list&search%5Bsex%5D=M&search%5Bage_class%5D=%25&event=MAR&favorite_remove=9TGG963828AFFB 

Women’s Results - 

https://results.chicagomarathon.com/2023/?pid=list&pidp=start 

Article on Kiptum’s Training- 

https://www.barrons.com/news/kiptum-s-coach-fears-intense-training-will-shorten-record-career-a94d00b1

item.title: E39 - Evolution in World-Class Endurance Training Practices
item.description:
An expert panel of 25 acknowledged sport scientists asked the following 2 questions: Q1 - What are the most important trends related directly or indirectly to the training and improved performance of the world’s best endurance athletes during the past 10–15 years? and Q2 - Which advances will contribute to further improving endurance performance during the next 10–15 years?

Dave and Matt discuss the questions and answers provided by the panel in this episode of Pro Running News.


Show Notes:

Article - Sandbakk, Ø., Pyne, D. B., McGawley, K., Foster, C., Talsnes, R. K., Solli, G. S., Millet, G. P., Seiler, S., Laursen, P. B., Haugen, T., Tønnessen, E., Wilber, R., van Erp, T., Stellingwerff, T., Holmberg, H., & Bucher Sandbakk, S. (2023). The Evolution of World-Class Endurance Training: The Scientist’s View on Current and Future Trends. International Journal of Sports Physiology and Performance, 18(8), 885-889. Retrieved Sep 6, 2023, from https://doi.org/10.1123/ijspp.2023-0131
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman

Dave and Matt discuss the questions and answers provided by the panel in this episode of Pro Running News.

item.title: E38 - Berlin Marathon 2023 - New WR and the Questions it Brings
item.description:
The 2023 Berlin Marathon bought world records, national records and some surprising results from the elites. In this episode Dave and Matt discuss the mens race, the womens race (featuring the World Record), the new supershoes on the market and more.


Show Notes:

Article on Running Economy changes with Weight of Shoe - 

https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/27327023/ 

Article Linking Running Economy changes to Performance Changes-

https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fphys.2019.00079/full

Articles Showing Inter-Subject Variability to the Same Shoes-

https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/abs/10.1080/19424280.2022.2038691

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40279-018-1012-3 

Article Suggesting Potential Differences between Biological Sexes in Response to Super Shoes- 

https://journals.physiology.org/doi/full/10.1152/japplphysiol.00002.2021 

Article Mentioned Regarding Running Economy Changes in Elites and Treadmill Impacting this-

https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s40279-023-01816-1

Japan Running News Tweet Mentioned Showing Pacing of 2 Fastest Wormen’s Marathons-

https://twitter.com/jrnheadlines/status/1707195568722964699?s=46&t=rG4B7Qh26_UR3jOG6o_6-w

item.title: E37 - Recovering from Endurance Events and Courtney Dauwalter
item.description:
Episode 37 of Pro Running News covers lessons from the ultra endurance world and Courtney Dauwalter's other-worldly efforts in the world's three most prestigious 100 mile races. Breaking record at Western States 100 & Hardrock 100 as well as winning UTMB - all in the space of 10 weeks!

item.title: E36 - World Championships Observations & Paris 2024 Looms
item.description:
We recap the World Championships, our observations, thoughts and take aways. We also discuss things to watch for with respect to Paris 2024.

We touch on Sport science, media coverage and of course training aspects of what we saw.

We recap the World Championships, our observations, thoughts and take aways. We also discuss things to watch for with respect to Paris 2024.

We touch on Sport science, media coverage and of course training aspects of what we saw.

item.title: E35 - Chicago Marathon Fields Deep Dive & Are Race Bonuses Fair?
item.description:
In episode 35 Dave and Matt discuss the fast Chicago Marathon fields and how both the mens and womens races might play out. They also question if the home-town bonuses are fair and good for the sport. Enjoy!

Pro Running News will no longer publish weekly - every monday. Instead, Pro Running News will now publish ad-hoc. Thank you for your support and understanding.

Chicago Marathon Fields Press Release

item.title: E32 - Velocity Based Training, Long Relays & Pre Race Pooping
item.description:
In this episode Dave and Matt discuss Velocity Based Training, Matt's experience at EuroTrip (the European version of The Speed Project - a 400km point-to-point road relay through 3 countries in Europe) and some recent research on the benefits of pre-race pooping.

Show Notes:
Article on Performance Post Defecation: https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/15502783.2023.2206380

item.title: E30 - Sifan Hassan's Crazy Racing Schedule Including Chicago Marathon, Balancing 5k/10k & Marathon
item.description:
This week Sifan Hassan annouced that she'll be lining up at the Chicago Marathon in the first week of October. The Chicago Marathon is just 6 weeks after the World Athletics Championships in Budapest where the reigning Olympic Champion will likely attempt to win two or three of the distance events on the track. Dave and Matt discuss the uniqueness of this racing schedule and how Sifan might approach training over the coming months.

Show Notes 

Chicago Marathon Women’s Field Announcement
https://worldathletics.org/competitions/world-athletics-label-road-races/news/chepngetich-hassan-sisson-chicago-marathon

Budapest Timetable
https://worldathletics.org/competitions/world-athletics-championships/budapest23/timetable 

Paris Olympics Timetable
https://www.nbcolympics.com/news/track-field-2024-paris-olympic-games

item.title: E29 - How Much Should We Worry About Running Technique?
item.description:
In this week's Pro Running News episode we answer a listener question from Jack about running technique and the importance of focusing on it in training and racing. Can technique be improved? And if so, how? Dave and Matt discuss.

Show Notes:
Paper on Fatigue and Technical changes
https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-023-35345-8 

Paper on Running Gait in Middle Distance Runners 
https://www.nature.com/articles/s41598-021-89858-1 

Paper Explaining Running Economy 
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4555089/ 

Paper Reviewing Modifiable Biomechanical Components of Technique 
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4887549/ 

Paper Suggesting Visual Technique Evaluation Doesn’t Correlate with Economy
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32930647/ 

Paper Showing Worse Running Economy when Concentrating on Altered Technique 
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/30307374/

item.description:

item.description:

Stay tuned for part 2 next week where the hosts discuss Creatine, Beta Alanine, Ketones and many more less commonly consumed supplements that may be beneficial for runners.

item.title: E24 - Why 7 day training cycles don’t make sense
item.description:
Most of us train Monday to Sunday with specific types of training sessions on specific days, for example - Intervals on Tuesday and Long Run on Sunday. Is there a better way to structure a training plan? Dave and Matt discuss this topic in detail on Episode 24 of Pro Running News.

We'd love it if you could leave a positive rating/review of Pro Running News on your podcast player if you've been enjoying it, thank you!

item.title: E19 - Painkillers, Russian drugs & Cannabis - Updates to Banned Substances List
item.description:
Dave and Matt discuss some updates to the banned substances list including pain killers, russian drugs and an amendment to the rules around Cannabis.

Show Notes: 

WADA List of Major Changes to the Banned Substance List:
https://www.wada-ama.org/sites/default/files/2022-09/2023list_explanatory_list_en_final_26_september_2022.pdf

WADA Full List:
https://www.wada-ama.org/sites/default/files/2022-01/2022list_final_en_0.pdf 

USADA Information on changes to the 2023 prohibited List:
https://www.usada.org/athlete-advisory/key-changes-2023-prohibited-list/

PAINKILLER & ENDURANCE SPORT
Paracetamol impacts on Endurance Performance:
https://www.mdpi.com/2075-4663/9/9/126

Painkillers and Sport Performance:
https://www.researchgate.net/publication/318722082_Analgesics_and_Sport_Performance_Beyond_the_Pain_Modulating_Effects

Endure by Alex Hutchinson (mention and reference to multiple studies on painkillers and endurance):
https://www.amazon.com/Endure-Curiously-Elastic-Limits-Performance/dp/153850202X

https://www.researchgate.net/publication/318722082_Analgesics_and_Sport_Performance_Beyond_the_Pain_Modulating_Effects

Endure by Alex Hutchinson (mention and reference to multiple studies on painkillers and endurance):
https://www.amazon.com/Endure-Curiously-Elastic-Limits-Performance/dp/153850202X

item.title: E18 - Are Ketone Esters the New EPO?
item.description:
Exogenous Ketones and the benefits are being studied closely right now and many professional athletes have recently discovered this new supplement and have incorporated Ketones into their diet. What are Ketones and do they really raise EPO levels? David and Matt discuss.

Show Notes:

Paper discussed:
https://journals.physiology.org/doi/abs/10.1152/ajpendo.00264.2022 

Paper Comparing Types of Ketones:
https://www.tandfonline.com/doi/full/10.1080/19390211.2023.2179152?needAccess=true 

Paper on Ketone Body Metabolism during Exercise: https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/JP273185 

Papers on Ketones and Muscle Glycogen Synthesis:
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5556006/ https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28588499/ 

Paper on Ketones and Attenuated Overreaching Symptom:
https://physoc.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/10.1113/JP277831 

Paper on Ketones and Appetite:
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC5813183/ 

Paper on Ketones as Signalling Molecules:
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC8922216/

item.title: E17 - Are All Elite Athletes Doping?
item.description:
Dave and Matt discuss doping again a couple of days after Zane Robertson's 8 year suspension was confirmed for testing positive for EPO. The two discuss Zane's interesting excuse, other interesting excuses provided by elite athletes upon returning positive tests and some interesting facts about the testing proceedures. 

Show Notes:

Article on Zane’s Ban
https://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/kiwi-olympian-zane-robertson-suspended-eight-years-for-doping/LOQE4ZZU6JEXFND2LAZF6HVGNI/ 

Article on Peter Bol’s Team
https://wwos.nine.com.au/news/athletics-news-2023-peter-bol-bernard-lagat-drug-test-controversies-lead-to-james-templeton-raising-alarming-question/b1e8114d-ea33-48f4-be37-355d8b85657d?ocid=Social-WWOS 

Articles on Testing for Synthetic EPO
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/18583375/
https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC6790592/ 

Article on WADA Testing Procedures
https://www.embopress.org/doi/full/10.15252/embr.202154431 

WADA Document on EPO Testing
https://www.wada-ama.org/sites/default/files/2023-02/td2022epo_v1.0_final_eng.pdf 

Podcast Episodes with Zane Post Announcement
https://open.spotify.com/episode/1PFFz2YqU73GPGu7kS4V8f?si=2DF7D815-3FF0-4040-9ECF-A354D2EAC307&utm_source=system-ua-share&nd=1 

https://open.spotify.com/episode/3sC8jQ2uQtSs4AnAq2JA1B?si=07A29292-D17A-4152-A90C-C6BA90B23DB9&utm_source=system-ua-share&nd=1 

Interesting excuses
https://www.casino.org/blog/10-worst-doping-excuses-in-history/
https://www.vegasodds.com/worst-doping-excuses-in-sports/
https://runningmagazine.ca/the-scene/ridiculous-doping-excuses/

item.title: E16 - Runners Aren't "Strong"
item.description:
In this episode: Dave and Matt discuss the terminology used for runners being able to back up and recover well between races and training sessions and why "strong" might not be the best way to describe most professional runners.

Show Notes:
Lisa’s World Athletics Profile:
 https://worldathletics.org/athletes/australia/lisa-weightman-14271094

Article with Summary of Durability and Training Intervention:
https://www.frontiersin.org/articles/10.3389/fphys.2023.1128111/full 

Article (paywall) on Durability:
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/33886100/ 

Article on Fat Oxidation Rate Differences between Well and Recreationally Trained Runners:
https://bmjopensem.bmj.com/content/1/1/e000047 

Article on Fat Oxidation Rate Differences between Well and Recreationally Trained Athletes:
https://peerj.com/articles/3769/#p-7 

Article on Training Status and Fuel Selection:
https://journals.physiology.org/doi/pdf/10.1152/jappl.1999.87.4.1413

Article on Adaptations in Muscle to Endurance Exercise:
https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/6373687/

Podcast on Durability Specifically:
https://scientifictriathlon.com/tts295/

item.title: E15 - The Norwegian System and Double Threshold Days
item.description:
Dave and Matt discuss the Norwegian approach to training (running and triathlon) and the inclusion of Double Threshold Days - what are they, who should consider incorporating them and more.

item.title: E14 - How to Fix Running
item.description:
Dave and Matt discuss some ideas around how to improve the sport (increase viewership, athlete salaries, popularity) of running via introducing more running teams and team events such as the Ekiden Relay's which is Japan's premier road running event each year.

Show Notes:

Ekiden: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ekiden

Sound Running Cross Country Event:
https://www.runblogrun.com/2022/11/teams-ready-to-clash-at-sound-runnings-fitness-bank-cross-champs-december-1-2022-from-world-athletics.html

Tempo Journal Article
https://tempojournal.com/article/the-spirit-of-the-ekiden/

item.title: E13 - It's Gotta Be The Shoes
item.description:
In Episode 13 of Pro Running News: Dave and Matt answer a listener question about elite runners and their use of modified/custom insoles. Dave is the man to ask questions about training and racing shoes and the rules at the elite level - so he gets stuck right into this one.

Show Notes:
World Athletics Shoe Regulations-https://worldathletics.org/news/press-releases/new-athletic-shoe-regulations-approved-2022

item.title: E12 - Should You Pull Out of Your Next Marathon?
item.description:
In this episode of Pro Running News Matt and Dave discuss the impacts of requiring to take time off during a Marathon build up. How much will time off potentially effect your end result at the Marathon?

Show Notes:

item.title: E11 - The Role of Managers in Running
item.description:
In this episode of Pro Running News we discuss Athlete Managers. What do managers actually do? What are their true responsibilities relating to athletes, brands and competition? And has their job become much more complicated with the new World Athletics points qualifying upgrade?

Show Notes:

item.title: E10 - Stepping Up To The Marathon
item.description:
Welcome back to Pro Running News. In this episode we discuss the current trend of 800m-1500m-5km athletes stepping up to the Marathon in light of the stacked fields at the London Marathon and Boston Marathon taking place in April.

Please review our podcast on Spotify or Apple Podcasts if you're finding the podcast useful.

Show Notes:

London Marathon Women’s Field
https://www.tcslondonmarathon.com/news-and-media/latest-news/greatest-ever-field-assembled-for-elite-womens-race 

London Marathon Men’s Field
https://www.tcslondonmarathon.com/news-and-media/latest-news/four-of-the-fastest-men-in-history-to-go-head-to-head 

Boston Marathon Women’s Field
https://www.baa.org/global-medalists-national-record-holders-olympians-and-paralympians-among-127th-boston-marathon

https://www.baa.org/127th-boston-marathon-mens-field-feature-abbott-world-marathon-major-champions-olympic-paralympic

item.title: E9 - Is the Marathon really 42.195km?
item.description:
In episode 9 of Pro Running News we discuss the topic of GPS and answer the questions: How are marathon courses measured? How are marathon courses run?
How does GPS work?


Show Notes:

Dissecting GNSS systems 
https://blog.bliley.com/the-differences-between-the-5-gnss-satellite-network-constellations
https://www.gps-repeaters.com/blog/gnss-vs-gps-whats-the-difference/ 

Comparing GPS to Other GNSS
https://www.educba.com/gnss-vs-gps/

Why GPS is sometimes inaccurate
https://www.gps-repeaters.com/blog/four-reasons-why-gps-is-sometimes-inaccurate/ 

Info on the GPS system
https://www.gps.gov/systems/gps/performance/accuracy/

Breakdown of Marathon Course Measurement

item.title: E8 - Strava - the good, the bad and the costly
item.description:
In this Episode of Pro Running News we discuss the recent increased cost of Strava subscription with a broader view of Strava.

Is Strava a training tool or social media app? How could they change their pricing model putting different features behind a pay wall? And of course the pros and cons of Strava as well as some recent privacy issues surrounding Strava. 

Show Notes

DC Rainmaker Article on Strava Price Changes:
www.dcrainmaker.com/2023/01/strava-raises-prices-doubling.htm 

Verge Article on Strava Price Changes:
https://www.theverge.com/2023/1/13/23553453/strava-subscription-increase-fitness 

Outside Article about Molly Seidel and Strava Privacy Concerns:
https://www.outsideonline.com/health/running/culture-running/people/molly-seidel-strava-privacy/ 

Sophie Mullins Tweet:
 https://twitter.com/runswritescode/status/1614277426745409538?s=46&t=JCS8dxLikgTkqAbxi0aedg 

Article on Impacts of Strava on Running Behaviour:
https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0378873322000909

item.title: E7 - Are World Marathon Major's Worth it?
item.description:
On Episode 7 of Pro Running News we discuss the World Marathon Majors. We look at the different levels of classification of road races, what is required for the different status’ given to events and look at the majors specifically. We discuss why you may or may not want to run a major and what some of the alternatives are. 

If you're enjoying the pod, we'd love a review. Thank you for listening.

item.title: E6 - Overtraining, Rhabdomyolysis and Collapse during a Marathon
item.description:
In this episode we discuss a listener question submitted, touching on a recent article from Outside Online. 

We touch on collpase and cardiac issues in marathons, rhabdomyolysis and precipitators and other factors involved in general overtraining. 

 

Show Notes:

Article submitted with listener question- 

https://www.outsideonline.com/health/running/culture-running/people/on-his-way-to-a-pr-a-marathoner-collapsed-at-mile-26/

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC4365849/

https://www.mdpi.com/2079-7737/10/5/362 

Overtraining - 

https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/pmc/articles/PMC3435910/ 

RED-S - 

https://bjsm.bmj.com/content/52/11/687

item.title: E5 - Are the new World Athletics Regulations for Qualification good for the sport?
item.description:

https://olympics.com/en/news/athletics-paris-2024-entry-standards

Announcement at 2022 USTFCCCA Convention Opening Session (53:50ish mark)- 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=5Nr1wGYt1b0

NCAA Drug Testing - 
https://www.reuters.com/lifestyle/sports/us-college-pro-sport-should-recognise-anti-doping-code-says-wada-chief-2022-08-02/

https://www.worldathletics.org/about-iaaf/documents/technical-information#collapsescoring-tables

 https://trackandfieldnews.com/seb-coe-talks-track-as-2023-approaches/

item.title: E4 - Valencia Marathon - did we see the new King and Queen of Marathoning?
item.description:
In episode 4 of Pro Running News - Matt & Dave cover the recent Valencia marathon & the results from the top end of the field. Specifically they examine & discuss the emergence of Kelvin Kiptum and breakdown of Letesenbet Gidey's race and what her future in the marathon holds. 

Kelvin Kiptum World Athletics Profile: https://worldathletics.org/athletes/kenya/kelvin-kiptum-14859609 

Letesenbet Gidey World Athletics Profile: https://www.worldathletics.org/athletes/ethiopia/letesenbet-gidey-14654918

Valencia Marathon Prize Money: 

https://www.worldathletics.org/competitions/world-athletics-half-marathon-championships/iaaftrinidad-alfonso-world-half-marathon-cham-7105083/news/press-releases/245000-prize-money-world-half-marathon-champi

Valencia Results: https://www.valenciaciudaddelrunning.com/en/marathon/2022-marathon-ranking/

item.title: E3 - Can Eliud Kipchoge win Boston?
item.description:
In Episode 3 of Pro Running News David and Matt discuss Eliud Kipchoge's chances of winning Boston Marathon 2023, what his plans might be over the coming years and his legacy.

If you're enjoying the Pro Running News podcast so far, we'd love a 5 star review. Thank you for tuning in. Subscribe to receive notifications of future episodes.

item.title: E2 - Where have all the rockstar runners gone?
item.description:
In Episode 2 of Pro Running News David and Matt discuss the media landscape in running and how it may be falling behind other sports such as Triathlon. They also discuss historical aspects of the sports why this may contribute.

Link to Ironman Documentary mentioned: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xA23ecuhB5w&t=2165s

item.title: E1 - Doping and the case against Kenya
item.description:
In episode 1 we discuss doping and the possibility of Kenya being banned by World Athletics this week.

Resources dissussed in the podcast:

Article on Potential Ban: https://www.aljazeera.com/sports/2022/11/25/kenya-faces-threat-of-athletics-ban-amid-doping-crisis

Article on Sharon Lokedi who won NYC Marathon: https://www.cbssports.com/general/news/2022-new-york-city-marathon-officials-admit-sharon-lokedi-was-not-drug-tested-before-her-victory/

Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU) Documents on Categorisation of Countries:

https://www.athleticsintegrity.org/know-the-rules/national-federation-anti-doping-obligations

https://www.athleticsintegrity.org/downloads/pdfs/know-the-rules/en/2021_01_01_Rule-15_Extract.pdf

https://www.athleticsintegrity.org/downloads/pdfs/know-the-rules/en/Criteria-for-Categorisation-under-Rule-15-PCH-TCD.pdf

Athletics Integrity Unit (AIU) List of Banned Athletes and Staff:

https://www.athleticsintegrity.org/disciplinary-process/global-list-of-ineligible-persons/

Document from the World Anti-Doping Agency (WADA) about Kenyan Doping:

https://www.wada-ama.org/sites/default/files/2022-02/Kenya_%20final_public_print%20%28003%29.pdf

Podcast Episode with Sarah Shibutse is the Director of Standards and Compliance at the Anti-Doping Agency of Kenya (ADAK):

https://cleancompetition.org/2021/09/21/62-the-anti-doping-agency-of-kenyas-commitment-to-clean-sport-sarah-shibutse/

  •

item.title: Welcome to Pro Running News
item.description: Welcome to Pro Running News. A podcast by Sweat Elite Founder Matt Fox and Medical Doctor and Exercise Physiologist David Lipman. We bring you running news in the elite running realm as well as deep dives into specific points of interest in the sport of running.
item.dc:creator: Matt Fox and David Lipman
